attachment.content:
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- rbk-wrangle: Muster, then roster RBK and JJK heats as two space-padded plain-text blocks
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Recipe Bottle Lode - lifecycle REST (guard-free cluster, sourced by rbld0_Lode):
20	#   divine — enumerate Lodes / inspect one Lode's members (read-only)
21	#   banish — delete a whole Lode (Director credentials)
22	
23	set -euo pipefail
24	
25	######################################################################
26	# External Functions (rbld_*)
27	
28	rbld_divine() {
29	  zrbld_sentinel
30	
31	  local -r z_touchmark="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
32	
33	  buc_doc_brief "Divine Lodes — enumerate all Lodes, or inspect one Lode's members (read-only)"
34	  buc_doc_oparm "touchmark" "Lode stamp to inspect (e.g., b260602120000); omit to enumerate all Lodes"
35	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
36	
37	  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
38	  test -f "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" \
39	    || buc_die "Director credential not found: ${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"
40	  local z_token=""
41	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
42	    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"
43	
44	  if test -z "${z_touchmark}"; then
45	    buc_step "Enumerating Lodes under ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/"
46	    zrbfc_list_anchors_capture "${z_token}" "${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}"
47	
48	    if ! test -s "${ZRBFC_PACKAGE_LIST_FILE}"; then
49	      buc_info "No Lodes found under ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/"
50	      buc_success "Divine complete — 0 Lodes"
51	      return 0
52	    fi
53	
54	    # Kind-letter legend, printed once so rows carry no repeated per-row column.
55	    # A touchmark's leading letter is its kind (b260602075327 -> bole); the
56	    # reader decodes the prefix from this key. One entry per implemented kind.
57	    local -r z_kind_fmt="    %-3s %-10s %s\n"
58	    echo ""
59	    printf "  Kinds (touchmark prefix):\n"
60	    printf "${z_kind_fmt}" "${RBGC_LODE_KIND_BOLE}" "bole" "upstream OCI image, consumed as a FROM line"
61	
62	    # Load the touchmark list fully before iterating: the per-Lode tags fetch
63	    # spawns curl (via rbuh), and a child touching stdin would consume the
64	    # loop's remaining input. Load-then-iterate keeps that FD closed.
65	    local z_touchmarks=()
66	    local z_touch=""
67	    while IFS= read -r z_touch || test -n "${z_touch}"; do
68	      test -n "${z_touch}" || continue
69	      z_touchmarks+=("${z_touch}")
70	    done < "${ZRBFC_PACKAGE_LIST_FILE}"
71	
72	    local -r z_row_fmt="  %-15s %s\n"
73	    echo ""
74	    printf "${z_row_fmt}" "TOUCHMARK" "IMAGE"
75	    printf "${z_row_fmt}" "---------------" "--------------------------------------"
76	
77	    local z_idx=0
78	    local z_pkg=""
79	    local z_pkg_encoded=""
80	    local z_tags_url=""
81	    local z_enum_infix=""
82	    local z_resp_file=""
83	    local z_image_file=""
84	    local z_image=""
85	    for z_idx in "${!z_touchmarks[@]}"; do
86	      z_touch="${z_touchmarks[$z_idx]}"
87	
88	      # One tags-list per Lode. IMAGE is the unsprued fingerprint tag
89	      # <sanitized-origin>-<sha10>; it is located via the sha10 taken from the
90	      # rbi_sha256-<hex> member tag, so Director semantic names (also unsprued)
91	      # cannot masquerade as the fingerprint. Per-Lode infix preserves each
92	      # response for forensics.
93	      z_pkg="${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_touch}"
94	      z_pkg_encoded="${z_pkg//\//%2F}"
95	      z_tags_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages/${z_pkg_encoded}/tags?pageSize=1000"
96	      z_enum_infix="rbld_divine_enum_${z_idx}"
97	      rbuh_json "GET" "${z_tags_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_enum_infix}"
98	      rbuh_require_ok "List tags for Lode ${z_touch}" "${z_enum_infix}"
99	      z_resp_file="${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_enum_infix}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_JSON}"
100	
101	      z_image_file="${ZRBLD_DIVINE_PREFIX}enum_${z_idx}_image.txt"
102	      jq -r --arg dp "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX}" '
103	        [.tags[]?.name | sub(".*/tags/"; "")] as $names
104	        | ([$names[] | select(startswith($dp)) | ltrimstr($dp)[0:10]][0]) as $sha10
105	        | ([$names[] | select((startswith("rbi_") | not) and ($sha10 != null) and endswith("-" + $sha10))][0]) // "(no fingerprint)"
106	      ' "${z_resp_file}" > "${z_image_file}" \
107	        || buc_die "Failed to extract fingerprint for Lode ${z_touch}"
108	      z_image=$(<"${z_image_file}")
109	      test -n "${z_image}" || buc_die "Empty fingerprint extraction for Lode ${z_touch}"
110	
111	      printf "${z_row_fmt}" "${z_touch}" "${z_image}"
112	    done
113	    echo ""
114	    buc_info "Total Lodes: ${#z_touchmarks[@]}"
115	    buc_success "Divine complete"
116	    return 0
117	  fi
118	
119	  # Inspect depth: list the member tags on one Lode package.
120	  buc_step "Inspecting Lode ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_touchmark}"
121	  local -r z_pkg="${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_touchmark}"
122	  local -r z_pkg_encoded="${z_pkg//\//%2F}"
123	  local -r z_tags_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages/${z_pkg_encoded}/tags?pageSize=1000"
124	  local -r z_tags_infix="rbld_divine_tags"
125	
126	  rbuh_json "GET" "${z_tags_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_tags_infix}"
127	  rbuh_require_ok "List tags for Lode ${z_touchmark}" "${z_tags_infix}"
128	
129	  local -r z_resp_file="${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_tags_infix}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_JSON}"
130	  local -r z_tags_file="${ZRBLD_DIVINE_PREFIX}tags.txt"
131	  jq -r '.tags[]?.name | sub(".*/tags/"; "")' "${z_resp_file}" > "${z_tags_file}" \
132	    || buc_die "Failed to extract member tags for Lode ${z_touchmark}"
133	
134	  if ! test -s "${z_tags_file}"; then
135	    buc_die "No member tags found under ${z_pkg} — Lode not present in registry"
136	  fi
137	
138	  echo ""
139	  printf "  %s\n" "MEMBER-TAG"
140	  printf "  %s\n" "------------------------------"
141	  local z_count=0
142	  local z_tag=""
143	  while IFS= read -r z_tag || test -n "${z_tag}"; do
144	    test -n "${z_tag}" || continue
145	    printf "  %s\n" "${z_tag}"
146	    z_count=$((z_count + 1))
147	  done < "${z_tags_file}"
148	  echo ""
149	  buc_info "Total members: ${z_count}"
150	  buc_success "Divine complete — Lode ${z_touchmark}"
151	}
152	
153	rbld_banish() {
154	  zrbld_sentinel
155	
156	  local -r z_touchmark="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
157	
158	  buc_doc_brief "Banish a Lode — delete the whole rbi_ld/<touchmark> GAR package"
159	  buc_doc_param "touchmark" "Lode stamp to delete (e.g., b260602120000)"
160	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
161	
162	  test -n "${z_touchmark}" || buc_die "Touchmark parameter required"
163	
164	  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
165	  local z_token=""
166	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
167	    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"
168	
169	  local -r z_pkg="${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_touchmark}"
170	  local -r z_pkg_encoded="${z_pkg//\//%2F}"
171	
172	  # Verify presence before delete so banish reports a clean not-found.
173	  buc_step "Verifying Lode present: ${z_pkg}"
174	  local -r z_tags_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages/${z_pkg_encoded}/tags?pageSize=1"
175	  local -r z_probe_infix="rbld_banish_probe"
176	  rbuh_json "GET" "${z_tags_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_probe_infix}"
177	  rbuh_require_ok "Probe Lode ${z_touchmark}" "${z_probe_infix}"
178	
179	  local z_tag_count=""
180	  z_tag_count=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_probe_infix}" '(.tags // []) | length') \
181	    || buc_die "Failed to count tags for ${z_pkg}"
182	  test "${z_tag_count}" -gt 0 \
183	    || buc_die "No Lode found at ${z_pkg} — nothing to banish"
184	
185	  buc_require "Will banish the whole Lode ${z_pkg} (single packages delete)" "yes"
186	
187	  # Single packages delete removes the package and all its member versions/tags
188	  # atomically. DELETE returns a long-running operation; trust 200 as accepted.
189	  buc_step "Deleting Lode package: ${z_pkg}"
190	  local -r z_del_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages/${z_pkg_encoded}"
191	  local -r z_del_infix="rbld_banish_del"
192	  rbuh_json "DELETE" "${z_del_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_del_infix}"
193	  rbuh_require_ok "Delete Lode package ${z_pkg}" "${z_del_infix}"
194	
195	  echo ""
196	  buc_success "Lode banished: ${z_touchmark}"
197	}
198	
199	# eof
200

content.text: Now let me verify several specifics. The findings cite line numbers and specifics. Note: the actual code has `buc_doc_oparm` at line 34 and the inspect branch at lines 119-150 (not 119-150 exactly, ends at 151). Let me check the spec files and the tabtarget trampoline.

content.content:
1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_lode_augur}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation inspects a single
5	{rbtga_lode}
6	— listing its member tags and decoding its
7	{rbtga_lode_vouch}
8	provenance envelope — by querying GAR directly via the Registry API.
9	Read-only; no local container runtime.
10	Authenticates as
11	{rbtr_director}.
12	
13	NOTE: Augur is the single-Lode inspect verb — the perceiving counterpart, at member grain, to
14	{rbtgo_lode_divine}'s
15	lifecycle-grain enumeration.
16	Divine answers which Lodes exist; augur answers what is inside one — its member tags and its decoded
17	{rbtga_lode_vouch}
18	envelope.
19	The fetched-side parallel of made-side
20	{rbtgo_ark_inspect}.
21	
22	NOTE: Augur is kind-agnostic.
23	It decodes the kind from the
24	{rbtga_touchmark}
25	leading letter and reads the same-shaped envelope every kind carries — so a new Lode kind needs no change here.
26	
27	The
28	{rbtr_director}
29	augurs a Lode:
30	
31	//axhopt_typed_parameter
32	{rbtga_touchmark}
33	. *Validate Input Parameters*:
34	.. {rbbc_require}
35	`«TOUCHMARK»` provided as the operation folio
36	.. {rbbc_require}
37	`«TOUCHMARK»` matches the touchmark format `«KIND»YYMMDDHHMMSS` (see
38	{rbst_touchmark})
39	.. {rbbc_fatal}
40	if malformed: "Touchmark format invalid"
41	
42	//axhos_step
43	. *Authenticate as
44	{rbtr_director}*:
45	.. Execute
46	{rbtoe_director_authenticate}
47	.. {rbbc_store}
48	`«DIRECTOR_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls
49	
50	//axhos_step
51	. *Fetch Member Tags*:
52	.. {rbbc_call}
53	GAR REST `tags.list` for the Lode package `{gar_lodes_namespace}/«TOUCHMARK»`
54	.. {rbbc_require}
55	at least one tag present — an empty package is the "Lode not present" signal
56	.. {rbbc_store}
57	`«MEMBER_TAGS»` — every member tag on the package
58	
59	//axhos_step
60	. *Decode Provenance Envelope*:
61	.. {rbbc_call}
62	Registry API: extract the `:rbi_vouch` manifest from the `{gar_lodes_namespace}/«TOUCHMARK»` package and read its `vouch.json` layer
63	.. {rbbc_store}
64	the
65	{rbtga_lode_vouch}
66	fields: `kind`, `acquired_at`, `acquired_by`, `capture_build`, `trust_grade`, and `members[]` (each `origin` / `digest` / `verification` / `tags`)
67	
68	//axhoot_typed_output
69	{rbtga_lode}
70	. *Display*:
71	.. {rbbc_show}
72	the kind (decoded from the touchmark prefix), the member tags, and the decoded provenance — origin, canonical digest, trust grade, and acquisition facts
73	.. {rbbc_show}
74	explicit trust-grade posture: `verified-against-published` versus `recorded-at-acquisition`, so the displayed claim never over-states what the upstream permits
75	
76	//axhoc_completion
77	Augur is a read-only inspection of one
78	{rbtga_lode}
79	via the GAR Registry API: it lists the member tags and decodes the
80	{rbtga_lode_vouch}
81	envelope, and modifies nothing.
82	+
83	The categorical layout makes the touchmark the primary index — every member tag and the provenance envelope sit within the one `{gar_lodes_namespace}/«TOUCHMARK»` package, so no cross-Lode aggregator is needed.
84	+
85	Trust posture is reported honestly per the envelope's declared grade: a `recorded-at-acquisition` Lode states that RB attests only the digest observed at capture, never that it remains re-checkable against a vanished upstream.
86

===rbw-ld trampoline===
#!/bin/bash
export BURD_LAUNCHER=launcher.rbw_workbench.sh
exec "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/z-launcher.sh" "${0##*/}" "${@}"
===RBSLD lines 10-25===
with the Lode's unsprued glance fingerprint as the IMAGE column.
Read-only.

NOTE: Divine is kind-agnostic.
A touchmark's leading letter is its kind (`b260602...` is bole); divine prints a kind legend once and decodes each row's kind from the prefix, so a new Lode kind needs no change here.
Divine is lifecycle-grain — it enumerates Lodes by touchmark.
The per-Lode inspect depth — member tags and the
{rbtga_lode_vouch}
envelope — belongs to
{rbtgo_lode_augur},
not here.

The
{rbtr_director}
divines the Lodes:

The findings mention `buc_doc_param` for banish (line 159) and `buc_doc_oparm` for divine (line 34) — confirmed in what I read. content.text: Confirmed: zero augur in any `.sh` or `.rs` file (grep exit 1, no matches). All augur references are spec-only. Every load-bearing claim holds. Let me note one small precision item on line citations before finalizing — the inspect branch is lines 119-151 (the finding says 119-150, which is the body before the closing brace; immaterial since stale-by-mount anyway). Let me submit.

verdicts.claim: augur does NOT exist in code — no rbw-la* tabtarget, no rbld_augur function in any .sh, no zipper enrollment, no theurge const; every 'augur' occurrence is in spec .adoc files only.
verdicts.note: Verified independently. `ls tt/rbw-la*` -> 'no matches found'. grep over Tools/ and tt/ restricted to *.sh/*.rs returns exit 1 (zero matches). The only 'rbld_augur' literal anywhere is RBS0-SpecTop.adoc:151 mapping line `:rbtgo_lode_augur: <<rbtgo_lode_augur,rbld_augur>>`. augur appears only in RBS0, RBSLA, RBSLD (all .adoc).

verdicts.claim: ONE verb (rbld_divine) covers BOTH grains: no arg enumerates all Lodes, a touchmark arg inspects one Lode's member tags; branches on whether BUZ_FOLIO is empty.
verdicts.note: rbldl_Lifecycle.sh:31 `local -r z_touchmark="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"`; line 44 `if test -z "${z_touchmark}"; then` opens the enumerate-all path (45-117), then line 119 comment 'Inspect depth: list the member tags on one Lode package.' opens single-Lode tag listing (120-150). Function closes at line 151. Citation of '119-150' is the branch body; immaterial.

verdicts.claim: The divine colophon rbw-ld is enrolled with param1 channel routing to rbld_divine; description states it covers both grains.
verdicts.note: rbz_zipper.sh: `buz_enroll RBZ_DIVINE_LODES "rbw-ld" "${z_mod}" "rbld_divine" "param1" "Divine Lodes — enumerate all, or inspect one Lode's members (read-only)"`. Exact match. z_mod is rbld0_cli.sh.

verdicts.claim: theurge consts define exactly three Lode colophons (ensconce/divine/banish), no augur const; RBTDGC_DIVINE_LODES is 'rbw-ld'.
verdicts.note: rbtdgc_consts.rs: RBTDGC_ENSCONCE_BOLE="rbw-lE", RBTDGC_DIVINE_LODES="rbw-ld", RBTDGC_BANISH_LODE="rbw-lB" — three, consecutive. grep AUGUR over rbtd/ returns nothing.

verdicts.claim: RBSLA describes augur as a separate fully-specified (not-stub) verb with complete body; RBSLD explicitly defers per-Lode inspect to augur.
verdicts.note: RBSLA-lode_augur.adoc is a complete operation spec: Validate Input (33-40), Authenticate as Director (42-48), Fetch Member Tags (50-57), Decode Provenance Envelope (59-66), Display with trust-grade posture (68-74), completion (76-85). Not a stub. RBSLD-lode_divine.adoc: 'Divine is lifecycle-grain... The per-Lode inspect depth — member tags and the {rbtga_lode_vouch} envelope — belongs to {rbtgo_lode_augur}, not here.' Confirmed.

verdicts.claim: Spec'd augur is RICHER than divine's current inspect branch: divine only lists raw member tags (jq .tags[].name); spec'd augur additionally decodes the :rbi_vouch / vouch.json provenance envelope (kind/acquired_at/acquired_by/capture_build/trust_grade/members[]). That decode is unimplemented in rbld_divine.
verdicts.note: rbldl_Lifecycle.sh:131 inspect branch does `jq -r '.tags[]?.name | sub(".*/tags/"; "")'` and prints MEMBER-TAG rows only — no manifest/vouch.json extraction anywhere in the function. RBSLA:60-66 mandates extracting the :rbi_vouch manifest, reading vouch.json layer, decoding the named fields, plus :74 the verified-against-published vs recorded-at-acquisition posture. Confirmed net-new.

verdicts.claim: Landing the split requires: (a) new tt/rbw-la.* trampoline; (b) buz_enroll line for rbw-la->rbld_augur param1; (c) RBTDGC_AUGUR_* const; (d) extract the inspect-one branch into rbld_augur(); (e) trim rbld_divine to enumerate-only; (f) ZRBLD_AUGUR_PREFIX in rbld0_Lode.sh kindle. Envelope-decode is net-new work to honor the spec.
verdicts.note: Mechanism corroborated. rbw-ld trampoline is exactly the 3-line BURD_LAUNCHER + exec z-launcher.sh form. rbz_zipper.sh enrollment pattern matches. rbld0_Lode.sh kindle block has readonly ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX / ZRBLD_DIVINE_PREFIX / ZRBLD_BANISH_PREFIX consecutively (55-63), so a ZRBLD_AUGUR_PREFIX slots in naturally. The inspect-one branch (119-150) is cleanly extractable. Envelope-decode confirmed absent.

input.corrections:
The recommendation stands; every load-bearing claim is independently confirmed. Three minor, non-load-bearing notes that do NOT change the recommendation:

1. Line-citation precision: the inspect-one branch the recommendation says to extract is rbldl_Lifecycle.sh:119-151 (line 151 is the function's closing brace); the findings/recommendation cite '119-150', which is the branch body. The function rbld_divine spans 28-151. Inconsequential and inherently stale-by-mount.

2. Step (4) recommends rbld_augur use `buc_doc_param` (touchmark-required) rather than oparm — this is well-grounded: the sibling rbld_banish (line 159) already uses `buc_doc_param \"touchmark\"` + `test -n \"${z_touchmark}\" || buc_die \"Touchmark parameter required\"` (line 162) as the required-folio precedent, and RBSLA:33-40 mandates require/fatal validation of TOUCHMARK. So the doc-param choice has a concrete in-file model to copy.

3. The recommendation's classification ('its own tiny standalone pace, leaning (b)/(c), not (a)/(d)') is sound. The one substantive nuance — already flagged by the agent — is the bifurcation of effort: steps 1-6 are a pure mechanical vocabulary/structural split of capability that already exists and works, whereas the spec-mandated :rbi_vouch envelope decode is genuinely net-new logic with no existing implementation to lift. Deferring the whole thing is mechanically safe (live `rbw-ld <touchmark>` already serves inspect), but leaves the documented divergence the agent named: RBSLD/RBSLA assign the inspect grain to augur while the live code keeps it on divine. All of this is accurately captured in the recommendation_input.

content.text: I've verified all seven load-bearing claims against the actual files. Every one holds. The recommendation stands as-is; my corrections are limited to minor non-load-bearing precision notes (a line-citation off-by-one on the function's closing brace, and supporting evidence for the doc-param choice via the rbld_banish precedent). The structured output has been submitted.
